criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking trade allocations as a distinct allegation category with its own dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations.' The output describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP and Chao's personal account while directing unprofitable or less favorable trades to other client accounts, including Crescent Bay Municipal Pension Fund and other discretionary account holders. It names the key individuals involved (Diana L. Chao and Gerald R. Whitmore), explains the mechanism of the scheme through omnibus account allocations, and references the applicable legal violations (Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act).

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly cites Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 in connection with the cherry-picking allegation multiple times throughout the document. For example, in the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, it states: 'This failure violated Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act by engaging in a scheme to defraud WCA's advisory clients and operating as a fraud and deceit upon those clients.' The same statutory provisions are repeatedly cited across multiple sections in connection with the cherry-picking conduct.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly and repeatedly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as the implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. The very first section is titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' and states 'the Division alleges that during the Review Period, WCA engaged in a pattern of favorable trade allocation commonly referred to as cherry-picking.' WCA is identified as the firm whose practices violated Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act through cherry-picking throughout multiple sections of the output.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Whitmore Identified as Implicated (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies Gerald R. Whitmore, CEO and CIO at WCA, benefited from the cherry-picking pattern through his 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP.' This directly satisfies the criterion.
